zh.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla是Erik的兒子，於大約1320年出生。他和NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla是Erik的儿子，于大约1320年出生。他和NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Eriksson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Nils, Per, Alf, Algot, Alvar, Bjorn, Elof, Knut, Mats.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Erik, from Old Norse Eiríkr (see Erickson ). Compare Erickson , Ericsson , and Erikson .

variants.name: Erikson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Anders, Nils, Astrid, Berndt, Iver, Karsten, Lars, Lennart, Lief, Thor.  German Gerhart, Ernst.

Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish   Eriksson  . This is also an Americanized form of the Norwegian, Danish, and North German cognate   Eriksen  . Compare   Erekson   and   Erickson  .

variants.name: Erickson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Nels, Thor, Nils, Lars, Sven, Lief, Anders, Lennart, Iver, Elof, Erland.

Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Ericsson and Eriksson , Norwegian, Danish, and North German Eriksen and Erichsen , or of any other Scandinavian or North German patronymic from a personal name derived from Old Norse Eiríkr, which is composed of ei ‘ever, always’ (or a shortened form of ein ‘one, only’) + rík ‘power’. Compare Ericksen , Ericson , and Erikson .
English (Lancashire): patronymic from the Middle English male personal name Eric (with the same etymology as 1 above) + son.

variants.name: Erekson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Thor.
Americanized form of Swedish Eriksson or of any other Scandinavian patronymic from a personal name derived from Old Norse Eiríkr (see Erickson ).

variants.name: Ericsson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Lars, Sven, Anders, Britt, Gunvor, Johan, Kerstin, Tor.
Swedish: variant of   Eriksson  . Compare   Erickson   and   Ericson  .

nameMeanings.name: Färla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Jose, Juan, Carlos, Enrique, Lourdes, Ana, Arturo, Cristina, Ernesto, Guillermo, Hernan.

Spanish and Catalan: probably a nickname from Catalan falla ‘torch’ or ‘bonfire’ (in particular one which was lit in the streets of a city on public festivals), or a metonymic occupational name for one who lit such a bonfire.
Spanish: perhaps also a nickname from falla ‘flaw, error, doubt’ (from Latin falla).

nameMeanings.name: Karl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of   Charles  , also used in the English-speaking world.  See also  Carl .

variants.name: Charles
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

variants.name: Carl
meanings: From an old-fashioned German spelling variant of Karl , the German version of Charles . It is now increasingly used in the English-speaking world, and for some reason is particularly popular in Wales.

en.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla was born about 1320 as the son of Erik. He had at least 1 son with NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld.

fr.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla est né environ 1320, fils d’Erik. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld.

de.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla wurde um 1320, als Sohn von Erik geboren. Er hatte mit NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla è nato il circa 1320 come figlio di Erik. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld.

ja.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färlaは約1320年にErikの息子として生まれた。彼はNN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköldとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla(은)는 대략 1320년 Erik의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla nasceu aproximadamente 1320 filho de Erik. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld.

ru.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla родился около 1320 г. Его отца звали Erik. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld.

es.lifeSummary: Karl Eriksson Färla nació aproximadamente 1320 como hijo de Erik. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con NN Nilsdotter Sågskura Kluven Sköld.
